RAILROAD WOULD


CLOSE BRQWNST.

N. P. Grade Separation Plans Sub


mitted to City.

pages.raw_text:
now used by the Great Northern


One street in the western part
of the city will be closed, although
if much opposition is shown, in
stead of closing Third av. alto
gether it will be changed to run a
block west and then pass under the
trestle.


The grade separation plan will
involve the expenditure of $4,000.
900. Just how soon the work could
be started is unknown, but at the
least it would not commence be
fore next summer.


A new depot, elevated to a height
of the tracks, which will be ele
waited by reason of the grade sepa-
ration, is necessary, and plans
have been made for it, but they
were not submitted this morning
Mr.. Cannon stated this depot would
cost approximately 100000 and is
to be built of stone and brick.


The city is not asked to bear the
least expense, provided the city
will accept unchanged the plans
which were submitted this morn
ing. When asked as to what would
be the result should the city in
sist that Brown st. be kept open
and other changes in the plans be
made, Mr.. Cannon shrugged his
shoulders and refused to hazard a
guess.


Councilman Baldwin, who with
N. S. Pratt and Captain John Gray
compose the council committee,
would not express himself at
length but stated that he antici.
pated the company would have
trouble over the proposition to
